title: Dependency Graphs, Incremental Builds, Caches, and Watch Mode · Runtime Lens: Clean Equivalence
summary: Changing a layout rebuilds one page, deleting content leaves output behind, cached nodes ignore parser versions, watch events duplicate builds, and a fast incremental result differs from a clean build. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the clean equivalence increment in clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Compare incremental and clean route manifests, bytes, diagnostics, removals, and digests across randomized change sequences.
Treat watch events as hints, debounce bursts, rescan authoritative state, serialize publication, and recover from overflow or missed events.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Clean Equivalence · Theory Clean Equivalence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 4193 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove dependency-aware incremental output is equivalent to a clean build; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-clean-equivalence — Compare incremental and clean route manifests, bytes, diagnostics, removals, and digests across randomized change sequences.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-incremental-dependencies-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-incremental-dependencies-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_incremental_dependencies_2(outputs: int = 20, dependency_nodes: int = 37, invalidated: int = 6, stale_outputs: int = 0, clean_digest_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if outputs < 1 or dependency_nodes < outputs:
        return False, dependency_nodes, "dependency-shape"
    if invalidated < 1 or invalidated > outputs:
        return False, invalidated, "invalidation-scope"
    if stale_outputs > 0:
        return False, stale_outputs, "stale-output"
    if not clean_digest_equal:
        return False, invalidated, "clean-drift"
    assert dependency_nodes >= outputs
    return True, invalidated, "clean-equivalent-incremental"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-clean-equivalence-17zoih
# Competency: ssg-clean-equivalence.
# Case 8475: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_clean_equivalence_17zoih(outputs: int = 20, dependency_nodes: int = 37, invalidated: int = 6, stale_outputs: int = 0, clean_digest_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_17zoih = "17zoih-hioz71-8475"
    assert evidence_variant_17zoih
    evidence_axes_17zoih = (
        "reader17zoih",
        "source8475",
        "parsehioz71",
        "route17zoih8475",
        "artifact847517zoih",
        "recoveryhioz718475",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_17zoih)) == 6
    if outputs < 1 or dependency_nodes < outputs:
        return False, dependency_nodes, "dependency-shape"
    if invalidated < 1 or invalidated > outputs:
        return False, invalidated, "invalidation-scope"
    if stale_outputs > 0:
        return False, stale_outputs, "stale-output"
    if not clean_digest_equal:
        return False, invalidated, "clean-drift"
    assert dependency_nodes >= outputs
    return True, invalidated, "clean-equivalent-incremental"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Clean Equivalence model · Theory Clean Equivalence
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Clean Equivalence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 6003 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove dependency-aware incremental output is equivalent to a clean build; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare incremental and clean route manifests, bytes, diagnostics, removals, and digests across randomized change sequences.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Clean Equivalence model · Theory Clean Equivalence.
steps.why: Retrieving Clean Equivalence through the concrete clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Clean Equivalence model · Theory Clean Equivalence, keep Clean Equivalence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare incremental and clean route manifests, bytes, diagnostics, removals, and digests across randomized change sequences.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Clean Equivalence model · Theory Clean Equivalence, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Clean Equivalence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Clean Equivalence model · Theory Clean Equivalence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 6003 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove dependency-aware incremental output is equivalent to a clean build; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare incremental and clean route manifests, bytes, diagnostics, removals, and digests across randomized change sequences.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Clean Equivalence model · Theory Clean Equivalence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A fast successful incremental build can be trusted without clean-build comparison.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-clean-equivalence-17zoih.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-clean-equivalence-17zoih(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_clean_equivalence_17zoih\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?outputs < 1 or dependency_nodes < outputs)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?invalidated < 1 or invalidated > outputs)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stale_outputs > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not clean_digest_equal)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
